wow_comparison.interpretation: All metrics improving. Assigned-analyst dropped from 165 (above operator_escalation threshold of 150) to 152 (still above but trending down — the analyst Mode 1 BAU drain is eating items faster than decider's BAU 3b is feeding. Net-velocity widened to +132/wk, meaning the system is closing nearly an order of magnitude more items than it's opening. Oldest open age zero — every status='open' has been processed. This is consistent with PROP-031 + PROP-032 working as designed.

findings.evidence: queue-history.jsonl: 2026-05-13T02:41 assigned_analyst=165; 2026-05-13T20:57 assigned_analyst=152 (net -13 in 18h). Closed-velocity-7d climbed from 133 → 148 (+11%). New-velocity-7d dropped from 28 → 16 (-43%). The drain rate is sustainable.
findings.suggested_fix: Continue monitoring. If next 2-3 tinker runs show assigned-analyst below 100, the operator_escalation/major thresholds clear naturally and no action is needed. If assigned-analyst plateaus above 150 with no further decline, escalate via HNOTE for analyst Mode 1 batch-size tuning. (Note: DIRECTIVE-20260513-002 — split analyst into baby + Opus variants — directly addresses this if the operator wants a structural rather than throughput fix; would be the next directive's PROP work.)

directive_outcome.title: Integrity's docs/index.html staleness check reads FUSE mtime but git is the actual published surface
tasks_completed: task_1_decide_the_authoritative_source — Recommended hybrid B+A (workspace-sync mirrors NEVER_PUSH docs/* clone → FUSE; integrity adds defense-in-depth git-HEAD cross-check). Operator's stated lean was option A; PROP makes the case for B as 'one-line for the high-frequency case + small loop for siblings + fixes the FUSE inconsistency at its source rather than papering over it for integrity alone'. The hybrid keeps option A's defensive flavor for the residual race window between a non-publish git change and the next workspace-sync mirror.

task_2_specify_the_implementation — Six concrete file edits specified: (1) workspace-sync.md Step 3 mirror loop (~10 lines) covering all four NEVER_PUSH docs/* files; (2) workspace-sync.md NEVER_PUSH array comment update; (3) structure-integrity.md Section 9 build-reproducibility cross-check; (4) structure-integrity.md Section 6 llms.txt content cross-check; (5) test.js HTML mtime-check → content-equivalence check (OPTIONAL — recommend deferring); (6) CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules note acknowledging the new clone→FUSE mirror direction.

task_3_audit_other_FUSE_vs_git_asymmetries — Built the comparison table (file × FUSE-update-mechanism × git-update-mechanism × integrity-checks-which × drift-risk × covered-by-this-prop). Identified four NEVER_PUSH docs/* files sharing the asymmetry: docs/index.html (HIGH risk, canonical case), docs/llms.txt (MEDIUM, count consistency), docs/sitemap.xml (LOW, URL list), docs/robots.txt (VERY LOW, static). Three other docs/* files (index.html.baseline, google verification HTML, fonts/) explicitly out of scope per ownership / no-integrity-check.

task_4_carve_out_what_NOT_to_do — Explicit preserve-list: file ownership model unchanged; build.js publish flow unchanged; workspace-sync universal-pusher and strand-rescue unchanged; decider commit flow unchanged; integrity primary checks unchanged. Explicit do-NOT-do list: not making integrity clone fresh; not making integrity web_fetch live site; not adding decider-side per-commit FUSE writes; not extending mirror to non-NEVER_PUSH docs/* files.
